visual_surface: AI accelerator supply stack: EUV tools, foundry/CoWoS, HBM, substrates, rack integration
chapter_value: Highest value because it explains why LLM compute is a physical supply chain, not just a GPU SKU.
allowed_caption: AI accelerators depend on layered upstream systems: lithography, wafer processing, advanced packaging, stacked memory, substrates, and rack integration.
blocked_claims: No claim these layers are complete, equally scarce, comparable in capacity, tied to one vendor, or sufficient to prove accelerator availability.
next_action: Build after source-page hashes and source-surface thumbnails are captured.

visual_surface: Advanced packaging bottleneck explainer: CoWoS/interposer/substrate/HBM package
chapter_value: Strongest single chip/fab mechanism visual after the stack diagram; it shows why memory proximity matters.
allowed_caption: Advanced packages place logic, HBM, interposers/substrates, and assembly into one constrained physical object.
blocked_claims: No CoWoS capacity, package yield, customer allocation, substrate shortage severity, cost, or supplier ranking.
next_action: Extract source-page screenshots and draft a neutral cross-section.

chapter_value: The visual texture is irresistible, but exact machine/photo rights are the hard part.
allowed_caption: An EUV/lithography image can show the physical scale of chipmaking equipment upstream of AI accelerators.
blocked_claims: No machine allocation, customer shipments, output capacity, price, export outcome, node eligibility, or AI-chip volume.
next_action: Keep A-0133 proxy until a machine-specific license and revision proof are captured.

chapter_value: HBM is a necessary visual in any serious accelerator chapter.
allowed_caption: Stacked memory became a named, visible product category in AI/data-center acceleration.
blocked_claims: No supplier rank, NVIDIA allocation, qualification, supply sufficiency, yield, price, availability, or market share.
next_action: Use a redrawn generic HBM stack with three official source cards in reserve.

visual_surface: Power-response taxonomy: nuclear, gas, grid, storage, transmission, interconnection, flexibility
chapter_value: Best Chapter 16 synthesis visual because it shows imperfect options side by side.
allowed_caption: AI/data-center load growth triggered multiple response categories, none of which alone proves solved power access.
blocked_claims: No response is sufficient, clean, cheap, fast, universally available, physically delivered to a named AI workload, or emissions-solving without same-scope evidence.
next_action: Build after final source cards for project examples are frozen.

visual_surface: Substation/interconnection physical image plus queue/source card
chapter_value: The grid bottleneck becomes real when readers see a substation beside an interconnection source.
allowed_caption: A substation image can make grid interconnection visible while the source card explains data-center power infrastructure.
blocked_claims: No site service, capacity, energized status, outage, delay, fuel mix, reliability, bill impact, or named-site operational proof.
next_action: Verify A-0139 license/revision; pair with A-0021 or Entergy source surface.

chapter_value: Transmission lines are less novel than substations but essential to speed-to-power.
allowed_caption: Transmission imagery can show that scaled computing depends on grid infrastructure and lead-time constraints.
blocked_claims: No capacity, congestion, queue causality, data-center access, reliability, or utility-wide conclusion.
next_action: Verify A-0140 and pair with interconnection queue schematic rather than a standalone photo.

chapter_value: Useful industrial drama, but named xAI/on-site claims remain permit-gated.
allowed_caption: Gas turbine imagery can illustrate speed-to-power tradeoffs in generation choices.
blocked_claims: No named-site deployment, turbine count, MW, runtime, emissions, legal status, fuel contract, or delivered capacity.
next_action: Use generic A-0141 only; acquire primary permit/operator source before naming any site.

chapter_value: Important because hyperscalers made nuclear procurement part of the story, but delivery claims are dangerous.
allowed_caption: A nuclear/cooling-tower image can illustrate the power-supply debate while source cards label PPA announcements as attributed claims.
blocked_claims: No delivered electrons, PPA fulfillment, restart success, SMR operation, safety, cost, hourly fuel mix, emissions outcome, or AI workload support.
next_action: Verify A-0143 license; use PPA source cards instead of company plant imagery.

chapter_value: Shows compute as room-scale infrastructure and helps readers feel the machine stack.
allowed_caption: Server-rack imagery can show compute becoming room-scale infrastructure.
blocked_claims: No named LLM training, GPU count, workload attribution, utilization, cost, owner-specific AI claim, or hidden hardware identity.
next_action: Verify A-0136 license and prefer neutral rack caption.

chapter_value: Good general industrial texture, but less evidentiary than grid/power surfaces.
allowed_caption: A data-center hall can convey the industrial setting of inference and training.
blocked_claims: No workload attribution, model training, energy use, owner identity, uptime, carbon intensity, or capacity.
next_action: Verify original Flickr license trail before final selection.

chapter_value: Useful for visible footprint and support infrastructure.
allowed_caption: A data-center exterior/cooling image can show visible support infrastructure outside the server room.
blocked_claims: No internal hardware, power draw, GPU inventory, workload, fuel mix, project economics, or operating status.
next_action: Keep as reserve behind substation/transmission/photo candidates.

chapter_value: Classic chipmaking texture, but generic cleanroom images can feel decorative.
allowed_caption: Cleanroom imagery can show semiconductor manufacturing as a controlled physical process.
blocked_claims: No named-fab AI-chip output, yield, capacity, customer allocation, node, or supply claim.
next_action: Use only if paired with EUV/packaging source context.

chapter_value: Simple physical texture; lower story value than package/HBM/EUV.
allowed_caption: A wafer photo can show chipmaking material substrate.
blocked_claims: No accelerator identity, node, yield, supplier, cost, production volume, or performance.
next_action: Use as reserve or small opener texture, not core exhibit.

chapter_value: Useful for response taxonomy, less central as a standalone visual.
allowed_caption: Battery storage imagery can show one grid-support component.
blocked_claims: No solved intermittency, named-site service, capacity, duration, emissions effect, or data-center backup claim.
next_action: Verify license; otherwise use a source card in the power-response matrix.

visual_surface: Clean procurement vs physical supply source card
chapter_value: Conceptually important, but existing A-0023 may already cover it.
allowed_caption: The visual should distinguish corporate clean-energy procurement from hourly physical electricity supply.
blocked_claims: No hourly fuel mix, PPA additionality, emissions accounting, or delivered-electron claim without matching data.
next_action: Do not add unless final layout needs another source-surface cue.

chapter_value: Important context, but not a photo and easy to drift away from LLM compute.
allowed_caption: Export-control documents can explain that advanced chips and manufacturing tools became policy-constrained inputs.
blocked_claims: No legal advice, effectiveness, smuggling prevalence, national capability, market impact, or shipment-denial inference.
next_action: Keep as sidecard near China/hardware prose if needed.

chapter_value: Likely too roadmap-specific for final 100 unless Chapter 14 needs future-node context.
allowed_caption: ASML framed High-NA EUV as a next-generation lithography system for advanced logic and memory.
blocked_claims: No claim it was required for a specific LLM accelerator, delivered capacity, or actual cutoff-era AI-chip output.
next_action: Keep as reserve; do not prioritize rights work.

chapter_value: Useful ecosystem texture, but less direct than TSMC/IBIDEN/HBM rows.
allowed_caption: OSAT/advanced packaging pages can show a broader assembly ecosystem.
blocked_claims: No specific CoWoS participation, customer allocation, package volume, U.S. reshoring outcome, or supplier rank.
next_action: Use only as reserve if package cross-section needs a third-source lane.

chapter_value: Can be atmospheric; evidentiary value is weaker than data-center exterior or nuclear tower.
allowed_caption: Cooling infrastructure can make heat rejection visible.
blocked_claims: No data-center cooling method, water use, heat load, site design, efficiency, or environmental impact.
next_action: Drop unless final spread lacks cooling texture.
